name: etetoolkit
tagline: SKILL.md wrapping the ete3 Python library for phylogenetic tree manipulation, orthology detection, NCBI taxonomy, and tree visualization.
verdict: No security fails. NCBI taxonomy download (~300MB) and optional sudo installs for Qt rendering are both clearly disclosed, not hidden or exfiltrating data. Minor hygiene issues: file runs 620 lines (over the ~500 budget) and lacks negative-trigger guidance and a unified output contract. Overall a legitimate, actionable reference for the ete3 bioinformatics t

highlights.summary: Lines 370 and 556 include 'sudo apt-get install python3-pyqt5 python3-pyqt5.qtsvg' for optional Qt rendering support; legitimate and disclosed, but a sudo command a user could run without scrutiny.
No explicit statement of when NOT to use this skill (e.g. non-tree hierarchical data, non-phylogenetic use cases).
No single unified output contract; outputs vary implicitly per capability (files written, printed stats, rendered images) without a consolidated statement of expected artifacts.
